Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu

Date modified:
Kristen Reichenbach, October 2018
ORGANIZATIONAL HISTORY
The core of the collection was assembled by Oscar Tschirky (1866-1943), known throughout the world as Oscar of the Waldorf. As maitre d'hotel of the Waldorf Astoria Hotel in New York City, from 1893 to 1943, he began a lifelong hobby of collecting menus, some of which were for functions he himself arranged, but most of which were of historical, gastronomic, or artistic importance. Upon his death, his menu collection, papers, and personal memorabilia were donated to Cornell University. The Hotel School Library has continued to add to the collection.
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
A collection of over 10,000 menus, providing evidence of the manners, mores, and food habits of earlier times.
Many menus include prints or half-tones of historic people and places. They may also include featured speakers, detail organizational leadership, and provide biographical sketches of the famous and infamous, including Theodore Roosevelt, Wilbur and Orville Wright, and Oscar Hammerstein. They may dramatize historical events, such as a "luncheon to the California Promotion Committee on the occasion of the resumption of the hotel business in the burned district after the earthquake and fire on 18 April 1906"; the opening of the first tunnels of the Hudson and Manhattan Railroad Company under the Hudson River; and a dinner in honor of Louis Kossuth, the Hungarian patriot. Prohibition era menus indicated the availability of spirits to their clientele by tucking a small insert into the spine cord of the menu.
Also menus collected by Charles N. Pinco.
Also, menus collected by Ruth Wood Green from hotels and nightclubs in the 1940s.

RELATED MATERIAL
See also the volume titled The Charles N. Pinco collection of international restaurant menus : gathered between 1909 and 1958 from the major portion of the civilized world, call # TX728.P56 + .

Scope and Contents
A bicentennial celebration of the Revolutionary War hosted by the mayor of Boston, Kevin H. White, at the Boston City Hall. The document includes the program of the event, the menu, and a guestlist. Finally, at the end of the document, there is a note that reads, "On July 11, in the year of America's Bicentennial, 1976, Her Majesty, Queen Elizabeth II and His Royal Highness The Prince Philip, Duke of Edinburgh, visited these Freedom Trail sites in Boston. Two hundred years after voices of liberty echoed through this historic city, we celebrate the strong friendship now shared by Boston and Great Britain."Boston, MABoston Globe "A Guide to Brunch," July 13, 19781978-07-13622Boston, MABoston Globe, "Dining Out," October 18, 19791979-10-18622Boston, MABoston Globe, "Dining Out," October 13, 19831983-10-13622Boston, MABoston Globe "How to Satisfy Food Cravings," May 31, 19841984-05-04622Boston, MABoston Globe, "Outdoor Cafes for all Reasons," July 26, 19841984-07-26622Boston, MABoston Globe, "Tasty Takes," February 5, 19841984-02-05622Boston, MABoston Globe Magazine, "What's Upstairs at the Pudding?", January 8, 19841984-01-08622Boston, MABroker's1979-08-08623New York, NYBuongiorno Kosher Daily Breakfast1997-03-16-1997-03-23562United StatesBuongiorno Lunch Menu1997-03-18562United StatesBuongiorno Welcome Gala Dinner MenuUndated562United StatesBrussels RestaurantUndated632New York, NYBurns Country Inn1974-04-24632The CabinUndated603Burnside, LACafÃ© Centro1997-05-09624New York, NYCafÃ© EscadrilleUndated603Burlington, MACafÃ© Iguana1990-03-23624New York, NYCafÃ© Louis XIV, Lunch Menu1948-07-02593New York, NYCafÃ© Louis XIV, Dinner Menu1948-07-02593New York, NYCamden Harbor Inn1984-08-18603Camden, MECamden Harbor Inn, Sunday Brunch Menu1984-08-19603Camden, MECandlelight Room1952-06-27593

Scope and Contents
This document is really several stapled together. There are multiple wine lists, and two letters to customers. The first, from November 14th, 1940, tells us that Hearns has been able to gather together a great quantity of French wine, which is now impossible due to the war. Apparently, "this is probably the most outstanding achievement in the history of 'America's Largest Liquor Store.'" The next letter, from 1942, describes its liquor as "safe from the ruthless hands of the invaders."Hearns Liquor Christmas Sale Advertisement1939-12-18597New York, NYThe Heritage Inn1983-10-23638Stanhope, NJHighlawn Pavilion1989-07-11629West Orange, NJHighlawn PavilionUndated629West Orange, NJHighlawn Pavilion1990-09-28629West Orange, NJHighlawn Pavilion1987-12-24638West Orange, NJHighlawn Pavilion1992-04-14638West Orange, NJHilton CafÃ© International1964-04-22-1964-10-18597

Scope and Contents
A bicentennial celebration of the Revolutionary War hosted by the mayor of Boston, Kevin H. White, at the Boston City Hall. The document includes the program of the event, the menu, and a guestlist. Finally, at the end of the document, there is a note that reads, "On July 11, in the year of America's Bicentennial, 1976, Her Majesty, Queen Elizabeth II and His Royal Highness The Prince Philip, Duke of Edinburgh, visited these Freedom Trail sites in Boston. Two hundred years after voices of liberty echoed through this historic city, we celebrate the strong friendship now shared by Boston and Great Britain."Boston, MABoston Globe "A Guide to Brunch," July 13, 19781978-07-13622Boston, MABoston Globe, "Dining Out," October 18, 19791979-10-18622Boston, MABoston Globe, "Dining Out," October 13, 19831983-10-13622Boston, MABoston Globe "How to Satisfy Food Cravings," May 31, 19841984-05-04622Boston, MABoston Globe, "Outdoor Cafes for all Reasons," July 26, 19841984-07-26622Boston, MABoston Globe, "Tasty Takes," February 5, 19841984-02-05622Boston, MABoston Globe Magazine, "What's Upstairs at the Pudding?", January 8, 19841984-01-08622Boston, MABroker's1979-08-08623New York, NYBuongiorno Kosher Daily Breakfast1997-03-16-1997-03-23562United StatesBuongiorno Lunch Menu1997-03-18562United StatesBuongiorno Welcome Gala Dinner MenuUndated562United StatesBrussels RestaurantUndated632New York, NYBurns Country Inn1974-04-24632The CabinUndated603Burnside, LACafÃ© Centro1997-05-09624New York, NYCafÃ© EscadrilleUndated603Burlington, MACafÃ© Iguana1990-03-23624New York, NYCafÃ© Louis XIV, Lunch Menu1948-07-02593New York, NYCafÃ© Louis XIV, Dinner Menu1948-07-02593New York, NYCamden Harbor Inn1984-08-18603Camden, MECamden Harbor Inn, Sunday Brunch Menu1984-08-19603Camden, MECandlelight Room1952-06-27593

Scope and Contents
This document is really several stapled together. There are multiple wine lists, and two letters to customers. The first, from November 14th, 1940, tells us that Hearns has been able to gather together a great quantity of French wine, which is now impossible due to the war. Apparently, "this is probably the most outstanding achievement in the history of 'America's Largest Liquor Store.'" The next letter, from 1942, describes its liquor as "safe from the ruthless hands of the invaders."Hearns Liquor Christmas Sale Advertisement1939-12-18597New York, NYThe Heritage Inn1983-10-23638Stanhope, NJHighlawn Pavilion1989-07-11629West Orange, NJHighlawn PavilionUndated629West Orange, NJHighlawn Pavilion1990-09-28629West Orange, NJHighlawn Pavilion1987-12-24638West Orange, NJHighlawn Pavilion1992-04-14638West Orange, NJHilton CafÃ© International1964-04-22-1964-10-18597
